title: Battery Module Analytics — Educational Models
disclaimer: Charts combine literature-calibrated models and simulations. They support aftersales QC reasoning (impedance spread, BMS balancing, cycle drift) but are NOT measurements from Durapower packs unless stated.

references.title: NASA Ames PCoE Li-ion Battery Aging Datasets (2008–2014)
references.use: 18650 · 2 Ah · V/I/temp · EIS (Re, Rct) — foundational open baseline

references.title: Devie et al. (2018) — Intrinsic variability, commercial 18650 batch
references.use: 51 cells · R = 73.2 ± 2.6 mΩ (~3.5% spread) at BOL

references.title: Gogoana et al. (2013) — Parallel cell resistance matching
Luh & Blank (2024) — KIT NMC/C-SiO aging dataset · Nature Scientific Data
references.use: 228 cells · OCV σ 0.002 V · EIS + 3B log points · prod. Nov 2020

references.title: Stroebl et al. (2024) — Samsung INR21700-50E multi-stage aging
Piombo et al. (2024) — Parallel module full-factorial DoE
references.use: NMC/NCA batches · HPPC R · interconnection R dominates imbalance

references.title: Zenodo (2024–2025) — Degradation path indicators, 48 commercial cells
references.use: EIS/NFRA BOL vs EOL · pulse · CCCV capacity

references.title: Hummes et al. (2023) — EV battery geometry comparison (MAUT)
references.use: Cylindrical vs prismatic vs pouch · thermal · mechanical · swelling

references.title: Battery University BU-301a — Types of Battery Cells
references.use: Jelly roll vs stacked laminate · swelling % · construction

open_datasets.note: Cell counts and years from publications. Use for comparing data richness — not direct chemistry equivalence.

generations.label: Legacy 18650 batch
generations.notes: R = 73.2 ± 2.6 mΩ; SOC spread on receipt up to ~8%

generations.label: NASA PCoE cells
generations.notes: Estimated from multi-cell aging variability; rated 2 Ah baseline

generations.label: LG HG2 (2020 production)
generations.notes: OCV σ 0.002 V on 3.556 V mean; tighter modern BOL uniformity

generations.label: Samsung 21700-50E
generations.notes: 279 cells · 3 replicates/condition · designed for variation analysis

generation_bol.trend_summary: Modern datasets (2022–2024) show tighter OCV/capacity spread at BOL than legacy 18650 batches, but NMC+SiO and fast-charge conditions introduce new fade modes (impedance, plating). Aftersales QC must track spread % AND chemistry generation.
generation_bol.capacity_cv_note: CV% = coefficient of variation at beginning of life from cited studies (some estimated from σ/mean).

fade_trajectories.note: Normalized illustrative fade — NASA curve = accelerated lab profile; KIT curves = moderate vs aggressive EV-like conditions (2024 dataset).

form_factors.label: Cylindrical
form_factors.construction: Jelly-roll wound electrode/separator on mandrel; steel casing; CID vent; cap/tab weld
form_factors.typical_chemistry: NMC · NCA · LFP (less common at pack level)
form_factors.durapower_context: Common in modular ESS strings, power tools, some EV sub-packs

form_factors.label: Prismatic
form_factors.examples: Samsung SDI · CATL · BYD blade (evolved prismatic)
form_factors.construction: Stacked or Z-folded layers in rigid Al/steel hard case; corner/seam welds; module compression frame
form_factors.casing: Rigid aluminum or steel hard case
form_factors.typical_chemistry: NMC · LFP (very common for ESS & commercial EV)
form_factors.durapower_context: Typical for LFP ESS modules and many EV pack rebuilds

form_factors.label: Pouch (pocket)
form_factors.examples: GM Ultium · legacy Nissan Leaf · many hybrid packs
form_factors.construction: Laminated Al-polymer pouch; stacked electrode sheets; tabs exit long edge; no rigid shell
form_factors.durapower_context: Lightweight EV/hybrid modules — swelling & compression QC critical

form_factors.score_note: 1 = weakest / hardest for aftersales · 5 = strongest / easiest. From Hummes 2023 + BU-301a synthesis.
form_factors.summary: No single winner: cylindrical excels in mechanical integrity and cell swap; prismatic balances space and is dominant for LFP ESS; pouch maximizes energy density per kg but demands compression frames and swelling management. Aftersales QC must match test protocol to form factor — not only chemistry.

shared_stack: Electrolyte + SEI formation during formation cycling

differences.cylindrical: Long strip wound on mandrel → jelly roll; uniform radial compression from casing
differences.prismatic: Stacked sheets or flattened wound roll; requires module compression frame
differences.pouch: Stacked laminate; relies on external stack pressure to prevent delamination

differences.cylindrical: Single tab pair (or tabless 4680) to cap; low tab length in 4680 designs
differences.prismatic: Multiple tabs to busbar; weld quality drives module IR spread (Piombo 2024)
differences.pouch: Tabs welded to pouch edge; pouch flex can alter contact under vibration

differences.cylindrical: CID + vent cap — predictable directional vent; decades of field data
differences.prismatic: Burst vent in hard case; module enclosure must allow gas path
differences.pouch: Pouch seam / weak point; gas swelling can crack module cover if unconstrained

differences.cylindrical: High-speed winding + automated grading; mature 18650/21700 sorting lines
differences.prismatic: Stack/weld in case; more variants; blade/cell-to-pack reduces module count
differences.pouch: Degassing & sealing critical; humidity ingress risk if seal damaged in service

swelling_vs_cycles.note: Illustrative thickness/swelling index (%). Cylindrical ≈ rigid casing (minimal). Prismatic & pouch require compression allowance — BU-301a cites ~8–10% pouch growth over ~500 cycles.

scenarios.cylindrical: Radial thermal gradient (hot core); good coolant access between cells in pack gaps
scenarios.prismatic: Flat-face cooling; contact pressure to cold plate critical as cell swells
scenarios.pouch: Less gradient-sensitive per Hummes 2023, but swelling reduces thermal contact under load
scenarios.aftersales_qc: Verify cold-plate contact & module compression torque after any module open

scenarios.cylindrical: Highest isotropic strength (Sahraei 2012); dent can still internal short via jelly roll collapse
scenarios.prismatic: Strong in compression axis, weak in shear/bend; case dent → tab weld stress
scenarios.pouch: Lowest puncture resistance; overlap pressure between cells can cause micro-puncture (Jiang 2021)
scenarios.aftersales_qc: Visual + IR + isolation test; pouch: reject if laminate crease, bulge, or seal damage

scenarios.prismatic: Cell + busbar weld IR adds spread; Piombo 2024: interconnection R can dominate imbalance
scenarios.pouch: Same as prismatic; flexible tabs add variable contact if compression frame loosens
scenarios.aftersales_qc: Measure HPPC/IR at module terminals AND per-cell where accessible

scenarios.situation: Calendar aging / ESS standby (LFP prismatic common)
scenarios.cylindrical: Stable form; low swelling; IR creep still tracked
scenarios.prismatic: LFP prismatic standard for ESS; monitor compression frame over years
scenarios.pouch: SEI growth + gas → visible swell; delamination risk if stack pressure lost
scenarios.aftersales_qc: Capacity + IR trend; for pouch/prismatic log compression shim thickness at intake

scenarios.cylindrical: Cell swap feasible; re-match IR within string; BMS relearn
scenarios.prismatic: Module-level repair; verify weld/busbars; harder single-cell replacement
scenarios.pouch: Must restore design compression; swelling check mandatory before ship
scenarios.aftersales_qc: Form-factor-specific gate: cylindrical=IR spread; prismatic=weld+IR; pouch=compression+swell

situation_behavior.chemistry_note: Durapower scope (NMC & LFP) crosses formats: LFP often prismatic/blade in ESS; NMC in cylindrical and pouch for mobility. Chemistry sets voltage window; form factor sets mechanical & thermal failure mode.

impedance_mismatch.note: Illustrative pack-level model for aftersales QC storytelling. At 20% resistance mismatch, literature reports ~40% lifetime reduction (Gogoana 2013). Small spreads (1–3%) have smaller but non-zero impact — BMS balancing load increases.

string_charge.note: Simulated CC-charge string voltages. Shows how BMS balancing limits ΔV growth when initial cell spread exists.

impedance_aging.note: Weak cell with 3% higher initial R — spread widens with cycling (aftersales risk).

nasa_sample.label: Representative 1A discharge (NASA PCoE-style 18650, illustrative)
nasa_sample.source: Curve shape for education; download raw .mat from NASA DASHLINK #133

key_messages: Modern cells (2020–2024 datasets) often show tighter BOL OCV spread than legacy 18650 batches — but Si-anode and fast charge add new failure modes.
1% R spread ≠ 1% efficiency loss in every pack — non-linear and C-rate dependent.
Literature anchor: ~20% resistance mismatch → ~40% cycle life at 4.5C (Gogoana 2013); ~3.5% IR CV at BOL in older commercial batch (Devie 2018).
KIT 2024: 228 cells, 3 per condition — use spread statistics in aftersales, not single-cell pass/fail.
Compare production date / chemistry generation when assessing repaired packs — same form factor ≠ same behavior.
Form factor drives failure mode: cylindrical = thermal gradient; prismatic = weld IR + swelling; pouch = compression & laminate integrity.
Aftersales gate must differ by construction: pouch/prismatic need swelling & compression checks; cylindrical needs string IR matching.
